commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=c358e924ea222a3c0ca2df5b5b47920ad6a98d15)
commands.review_summary:
Adds Codex/OpenAI auth-shaped strings to the shared failover matcher, preserves formatted assistant error text as assistant failover rawError, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +26, Tests +90. Total +116 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main classifies assistant auth/failover from assistant.errorMessage in run.ts before handleAssistantFailover sees formatted text. I did not run the live dashboard/provider fallback path in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor branch updates and branch-specific real behavior proof; ClawSweeper should not queue an automated repair while the proof gap is tied to the contributor's local hotfix setup.

target.title: fix(model-fallback): classify Codex/OpenAI auth failures
target.body:
## Summary

Draft fix for #93272.

This PR improves model failover for Codex/OpenAI auth-shaped failures by:

- classifying `auth_unavailable`, `authentication_error`, and invalidated-token text as auth failures;
- preserving formatted assistant error text as `FailoverError.rawError` when the assistant has no raw `errorMessage`;
- using the formatted text for short-window rate-limit retry checks when raw error text is absent.

## Why

A local OpenClaw v2026.6.6 incident showed a failed primary model request did not advance to the next configured provider when the upstream error text was an auth-shaped Codex/OpenAI payload. The local hotfix was validated against the affected `heme-literature` dashboard session: primary `openai/gpt-5.5` failed, then fallback `bigmodel/glm-5.2` returned `OK`.

## Redacted Behavior Proof

Source: local installed OpenClaw hotfix, affected dashboard session `agent:heme-literature:dashboard:3d5a1f06-de8c-45d9-8526-aefc77144cd0`, session id `d01a0fd4-2616-4f64-ba49-66cba90faadb`. Secrets, prompts, paths, and config payloads omitted.

```jsonl
{"ts":"2026-06-15T11:16:51.491Z","type":"session.started","runId":"79873b82-1f45-41a0-8fc0-0b19a010c694","provider":"openai","modelId":"gpt-5.5","modelApi":"openai-responses","authProfileId":"openai:api-key"}
{"ts":"2026-06-15T11:17:27.146Z","type":"session.ended","runId":"79873b82-1f45-41a0-8fc0-0b19a010c694","provider":"openai","modelId":"gpt-5.5","modelApi":"openai-responses","status":"error"}
{"ts":"2026-06-15T11:17:28.608Z","type":"session.started","runId":"79873b82-1f45-41a0-8fc0-0b19a010c694","provider":"bigmodel","modelId":"glm-5.2","modelApi":"openai-completions"}
{"ts":"2026-06-15T11:17:45.230Z","type":"trace.artifacts","runId":"79873b82-1f45-41a0-8fc0-0b19a010c694","provider":"bigmodel","modelId":"glm-5.2","modelApi":"openai-completions","status":"success","assistantTexts":["OK"]}
{"ts":"2026-06-15T11:17:45.231Z","type":"session.ended","runId":"79873b82-1f45-41a0-8fc0-0b19a010c694","provider":"bigmodel","modelId":"glm-5.2","modelApi":"openai-completions","status":"success"}
```

Additional same-session fallback-chain proof after `bigmodel/glm-5.2` later hit quota: `openai/gpt-5.5` ended `error`, `bigmodel/glm-5.2` ended `error`, and `deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro` ended `success` in run `f13a5676-91e8-4aa8-a818-f4908cad690a`.

## Tests

- Added matcher regression coverage for Codex/OpenAI auth payloads.
- Added focused assistant-failover coverage for missing raw `errorMessage`.
- Local installed OpenClaw hotfix was smoke-tested with the affected agent/session.

## Draft note

The local hotfix also includes a larger `run.ts` caller-side enrichment patch so assistant-stage classification sees formatted error text before it chooses a failover action. That larger file update is still local-only because my local git credentials are unavailable and pushing that 170KB file through the connector is impractical. This draft carries the narrower upstreamable pieces first.

The broader zero-output assistant, session/model override, and auth-order policy details remain tracked in #93272 and should not be considered closed by merging this narrower PR alone.
